        "contents": "from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\r\nimport datetime\r\n\r\n\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterFactory, DataAdapterSelector\r\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import LeagueQuery, TeamSeasonPerformance, LeagueStandings, TeamWeeklyPerformance\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service\r\nfrom app.models.table_data import TableData, ColumnGroup, Column, PlotData, TileData\r\nfrom app.services.statistics_service import StatisticsService\r\nfrom app.models.statistics_models import LeagueStatistics, LeagueResults\r\nfrom app.models.series_data import SeriesData\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\nfrom itertools import accumulate\r\n# from data_access.series_data import calculate_series_data, get_player_series_data, get_team_series_data\r\n\r\n\r\nclass LeagueService:\r\n    def __init__(self, adapter_type=DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS):\r\n        self.adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(adapter_type)\r\n        self.stats_service = StatisticsService()\r\n\r\n    def get_available_weeks(self, season: str, league: str) -\u003e List[int]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get available weeks for a season and league\"\"\"\r\n        return self.adapter.get_weeks(season, league)\r\n    \r\n    def get_latest_week(self, season: str, league: str) -\u003e int:\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest week number for a season and league\"\"\"\r\n        weeks = self.get_available_weeks(season, league)\r\n        return max(weeks) if weeks else 1\r\n    \r\n    def get_seasons(self) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get all available seasons\"\"\"\r\n        return self.adapter.get_seasons()\r\n\r\n    def get_leagues(self) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get all available leagues for a season\"\"\"\r\n        return self.adapter.get_leagues()",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_teams')\r\ndef get_available_teams():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": \"Season and league are required\"}), 400\r\n            \r\n        teams = league_service.get_teams_in_league_season(league, season)\r\n\r\n        return jsonify(teams)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

        "contents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\nleague_service = LeagueService()\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/stats')\r\ndef stats():\r\n    try:\r\n        weeks = league_service.get_weeks()\r\n        print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n        return render_template('league/stats.html', \r\n                            season=league_service.get_seasons(),\r\n                            league=league_service.get_leagues(),\r\n                            week=weeks)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in stats route: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

        ],
        "originalContents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\nleague_service = LeagueService()\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/stats')\r\ndef stats():\r\n    try:\r\n        weeks = league_service.get_weeks()\r\n        print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n        return render_template('league/stats.html', \r\n                            season=league_service.get_seasons(),\r\n                            league=league_service.get_leagues(),\r\n                            week=weeks)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in stats route: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_week_table')\r\ndef get_league_week_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        # week should be int\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        # print(\"get_league_week\")\r\n        print(f\"League Standings - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": i18n_service.get_text(\"season_league_required\")}), 400\r\n\r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        # This now returns a TableData object\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_week_table_simple(season=season, league=league, week=week)\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        #print(\"league_routes.get_league_week_table: \", end=\"\")\r\n        #print(table_data)\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_league_week: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")  # This will show the full error trace\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_week_table')\r\ndef get_league_week_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        # week should be int\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        # print(\"get_league_week\")\r\n        print(f\"League Standings - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": i18n_service.get_text(\"season_league_required\")}), 400\r\n\r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        # This now returns a TableData object\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_week_table_simple(season=season, league=league, week=week)\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        #print(\"league_routes.get_league_week_table: \", end=\"\")\r\n        #print(table_data)\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_league_week: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")  # This will show the full error trace\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_valid_combinations(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Dict[str, List[str]]]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get valid combinations of season, league, and weeks\"\"\"\r\n        # This is a placeholder implementation - adjust based on your data structure\r\n        result = {}\r\n        seasons = self.get_seasons()\r\n        \r\n        for season in seasons:\r\n            result[season] = {}\r\n            leagues = self.get_leagues()\r\n            \r\n            for league in leagues:\r\n                weeks = self.get_weeks(league_name=league, season=season)\r\n                if weeks:\r\n                    result[season][league] = weeks\r\n        \r\n        return result\r\n\r\n    def get_teams_in_league_season(self, league: str, season: str) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get teams in a specific league and season\"\"\"\r\n        # Create a query to get all teams in this league and season\r\n        query = LeagueQuery(season=season, league=league)\r\n        \r\n        # Get the data\r\n        league_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(query.to_filter_dict())\r\n        \r\n        if league_data.empty:\r\n            return []\r\n        \r\n        # Extract unique team names\r\n        if 'Team' in league_data.columns:\r\n            teams = sorted(league_data['Team'].unique().tolist())\r\n            return teams\r\n        \r\n        return []",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_valid_combinations(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Dict[str, List[str]]]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get valid combinations of season, league, and weeks\"\"\"\r\n        # This is a placeholder implementation - adjust based on your data structure\r\n        result = {}\r\n        seasons = self.get_seasons()\r\n        \r\n        for season in seasons:\r\n            result[season] = {}\r\n            leagues = self.get_leagues()\r\n            \r\n            for league in leagues:\r\n                weeks = self.get_weeks(league_name=league, season=season)\r\n                if weeks:\r\n                    result[season][league] = weeks\r\n        \r\n        return result\r\n\r\n    def get_teams_in_league_season(self, league: str, season: str) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get teams in a specific league and season\"\"\"\r\n        # Create a query to get all teams in this league and season\r\n        query = LeagueQuery(season=season, league=league)\r\n        \r\n        # Get the data\r\n        league_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(query.to_filter_dict())\r\n        \r\n        if league_data.empty:\r\n            return []\r\n        \r\n        # Extract unique team names\r\n        if 'Team' in league_data.columns:\r\n            teams = sorted(league_data['Team'].unique().tolist())\r\n            return teams\r\n        \r\n        return []",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_details')\r\ndef get_team_week_details():\r\n    \r\n    season = request.args.get('season')\r\n    week = request.args.get('week')\r\n    team = request.args.get('team')\r\n    league = request.args.get('league')\r\n    \r\n    print(f\"Team Week Details - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}, team={team}\")\r\n\r\n    week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n    \r\n    details = league_service.get_team_week_details(league, season, team, week)\r\n    print(details)\r\n    \r\n    return jsonify({'config': details})",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_details')\r\ndef get_team_week_details():\r\n    \r\n    season = request.args.get('season')\r\n    week = request.args.get('week')\r\n    team = request.args.get('team')\r\n    league = request.args.get('league')\r\n    \r\n    print(f\"Team Week Details - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}, team={team}\")\r\n\r\n    week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n    \r\n    details = league_service.get_team_week_details(league, season, team, week)\r\n    print(details)\r\n    \r\n    return jsonify({'config': details})",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_league_performance_chart(self, season: str, league: str, team_id: Optional[str] = None) -\u003e PlotData:\n\r\n        \r\n        ...\n\r\n        \r\n        # Weeks completed tile\r\n        if standings.teams and standings.teams[0].weekly_performances:\r\n            completed_weeks = len(set(p.week for p in standings.teams[0].weekly_performances))\r\n            tiles.append(\r\n                TileData(\r\n                    title=\"Weeks Completed\",\r\n                    value=str(completed_weeks),\r\n                    subtitle=f\"of {completed_weeks + 2}\",  # Assuming 2 more weeks to go\r\n                    type=\"progress\",\r\n                    chart_data=[completed_weeks, completed_weeks + 2]\r\n                )\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        return tiles\r\n    \r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit: int = 5) -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get the latest league events.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            limit: Maximum number of events to return\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            List of event dictionaries\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        # Get all events from the adapter\r\n        events_df = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            filters={},\r\n            sort_by=\"Date\",\r\n            ascending=False,\r\n            limit=limit\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        # If no events, return empty list\r\n        if events_df.empty:\r\n            return []\r\n        \r\n        # Convert to list of dictionaries\r\n        events = []\r\n        for _, row in events_df.iterrows():\r\n            event = {}\r\n            for col in [\"Season\", \"League\", \"Week\", \"Date\"]:\r\n                if col in row:\r\n                    event[col] = row[col]\r\n            events.append(event)\r\n        \r\n        return events\r\n\r\n    def get_weeks(self, league_name: str = None, season: str = None) -\u003e List[int]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get available weeks for a league and season\"\"\"\r\n        #if not league_name or not season:\r\n            # Return empty list if parameters are missing\r\n        #    return []\r\n        return self.adapter.get_weeks(season, league_name)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_details_table')\r\ndef get_team_week_details_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        team = request.args.get('team')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"Team Week Details Table - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}, team={team}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not all([season, league, week, team]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': i18n_service.get_text('missing_parameters')}), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_team_week_details_table_data(\r\n            league=league, \r\n            season=season, \r\n            team=team, \r\n            week=week\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        print(table_data)\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_week_details_table: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "\r"
          },

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_details_table')\r\ndef get_team_week_details_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        team = request.args.get('team')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"Team Week Details Table - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}, team={team}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not all([season, league, week, team]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': i18n_service.get_text('missing_parameters')}), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_team_week_details_table_data(\r\n            league=league, \r\n            season=season, \r\n            team=team, \r\n            week=week\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        print(table_data)\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_week_details_table: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 47,
            "line": 176
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 40,

          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_league_week_table(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, depth: int = 1) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get a formatted table for league standings with weekly breakdowns.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\r\n            depth: How many previous weeks to include\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            TableData object with the league standings\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        # If week is not specified, get the latest week\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\r\n        \r\n        # Get team performances from the adapter\r\n        team_performances = self.adapter.get_league_standings(season, league, week)\r\n        \r\n        if not team_performances:\r\n            # Return empty table structure\r\n            return TableData(\r\n                columns=[],\r\n                data=[],\r\n                title=f\"No data available for {league} - {season}\"\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        # Sort by total points (descending) and assign positions\r\n        team_performances.sort(key=lambda x: (x.total_points, x.total_score), reverse=True)\r\n        for i, perf in enumerate(team_performances, 1):\r\n            perf.position = i\r\n        \r\n        # Create column groups\r\n        columns = [\r\n            ColumnGroup(\r\n                title=\"Ranking\",\r\n                frozen=\"left\",  # Freeze this group to the left\r\n                style={\"backgroundColor\": \"#f8f9fa\"},  # Light gray background\r\n                columns=[\r\n                    Column(title=\"#\", field=\"pos\", width=\"50px\", align=\"center\"),\r\n                    Column(title=\"Team\", field=\"team\", width=\"200px\", align=\"left\")\r\n                ]\r\n            )\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        # Add columns for each week\r\n        start_week = max(1, week - depth + 1)",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_league_week_table(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, depth: int = 1) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get a formatted table for league standings with weekly breakdowns.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\r\n            depth: How many previous weeks to include\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            TableData object with the league standings\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        # If week is not specified, get the latest week\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\r\n        \r\n        # Get team performances from the adapter\r\n        team_performances = self.adapter.get_league_standings(season, league, week)\r\n        \r\n        if not team_performances:\r\n            # Return empty table structure\r\n            return TableData(\r\n                columns=[],\r\n                data=[],\r\n                title=f\"No data available for {league} - {season}\"\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        # Sort by total points (descending) and assign positions\r\n        team_performances.sort(key=lambda x: (x.total_points, x.total_score), reverse=True)\r\n        for i, perf in enumerate(team_performances, 1):\r\n            perf.position = i\r\n        \r\n        # Create column groups\r\n        columns = [\r\n            ColumnGroup(\r\n                title=\"Ranking\",\r\n                frozen=\"left\",  # Freeze this group to the left\r\n                style={\"backgroundColor\": \"#f8f9fa\"},  # Light gray background\r\n                columns=[\r\n                    Column(title=\"#\", field=\"pos\", width=\"50px\", align=\"center\"),\r\n                    Column(title=\"Team\", field=\"team\", width=\"200px\", align=\"left\")\r\n                ]\r\n            )\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        # Add columns for each week\r\n        start_week = max(1, week -         "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e LeagueStandings:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get league standings for a specific season, league, and week.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The week number (if None, gets the latest week)\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            LeagueStandings object with team performances\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        # If week is not specified, get the latest week\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\r\n        \r\n        print(\"week: \", week)\r\n\r\n        # Get league statistics\r\n        stats = self.stats_service.get_league_statistics(league, season)\r\n        if not stats:\r\n            return LeagueStandings(\r\n                season=season,\r\n                league_name=league,\r\n                week=week,\r\n                teams=[],\r\n                last_updated=datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        # Convert statistics to team performances\r\n        team_performances = []",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e LeagueStandings:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get league standings for a specific season, league, and week.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The week number (if None, gets the latest week)\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            LeagueStandings object with team performances\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        # If week is not specified, get the latest week\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\r\n        \r\n        print(\"week: \", week)\r\n\r\n        # Get league statistics\r\n        stats = self.stats_service.get_league_statistics(league, season)\r\n        if not stats:\r\n            return LeagueStandings(\r\n                season=season,\r\n                league_name=league,\r\n                week=week,\r\n                teams=[],\r\n                last_updated=datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        # Convert statistics to team performances\r\n        team_performances = []",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_positions')\r\ndef get_team_positions():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n           \r\n\r\n        print(f\"Team Positions - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': i18n_service.get_text('missing_parameters')}), 400\r\n            \r\n        positions = league_service.get_team_positions_simple(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        #print(positions)\r\n        return jsonify(positions)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_positions: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_positions')\r\ndef get_team_positions():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n           \r\n\r\n        print(f\"Team Positions - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': i18n_service.get_text('missing_parameters')}), 400\r\n            \r\n        positions = league_service.get_team_positions_simple(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        #print(positions)\r\n        return jsonify(positions)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_positions: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_league_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, include_history: bool = False) -\u003e TableData:\n\r\n            \r\n            ...\n\r\n            \r\n            if league_data.empty:\r\n                return TableData(\r\n                    columns=[],\r\n                    data=[],\r\n                    title=f\"No data available for {league} - {season}\"\r\n                )\r\n            \r\n            # Get als and weeks\r\n            teams = league_data[Columns.team_name].unique()\r\n            all_weeks = sorted(league_data[Columns.week].unique())\r\n            \r\n            # Determine which weeks to show\r\n            if include_history:\r\n                weeks_to_show = [w for w in all_weeks if w \u003c= week]\r\n            else:\r\n                weeks_to_show = [week]\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate data for each team\r\n            team_data = {}\r\n            \r\n            for team in teams:\r\n                team_data[team] = {\r\n                    'weekly_data': {},\r\n                    'season_score': 0,\r\n                    'season_points': 0,\r\n                    'season_avg': 0\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                # Get individual player data for this team (for scores and averages)\r\n                team_individual_data = individual_data[individual_data[Columns.team_name] == team]\r\n                \r\n                # Get team bonus data for this team (for team points only)\r\n                team_bonus_team_data = team_bonus_data[team_bonus_data[Columns.team_name] == team]",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table')\r\ndef get_team_week_head_to_head_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        week_str = request.args.get('week')\r\n        team = request.args.get('team')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"Team Week Head-to-Head Table - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week_str}, team={team}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not all([season, league, week_str, team]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        week = int(week_str)\r\n        \r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(\r\n            league=league, \r\n            season=season, \r\n            team=team, \r\n            week=week\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        print(table_data)\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_week_head_to_head_table: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table')\r\ndef get_team_week_head_to_head_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        week_str = request.args.get('week')\r\n        team = request.args.get('team')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"Team Week Head-to-Head Table - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week_str}, team={team}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not all([season, league, week_str, team]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        week = int(week_str)\r\n        \r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(\r\n            league=league, \r\n            season=season, \r\n            team=team, \r\n            week=week\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        print(table_data)\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_week_head_to_head_table: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team week details as a TableData object for rendering\"\"\"\r\n        try:\r\n            # Get individual player results for the team\r\n            player_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            player_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=player_filters)\r\n            \r\n            if player_data.empty:\r\n                return TableData(\r\n                    columns=[],\r\n                    data=[],\r\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\r\n                )\r\n            \r\n            # Get team totals (computed data)\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            # Get opponent team totals\r\n            opponent_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team week details as a TableData object for rendering\"\"\"\r\n        try:\r\n            # Get individual player results for the team\r\n            player_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            player_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=player_filters)\r\n            \r\n            if player_data.empty:\r\n                return TableData(\r\n                    columns=[],\r\n                    data=[],\r\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\r\n                )\r\n            \r\n            # Get team totals (computed data)\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            # Get opponent team totals\r\n            opponent_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get details for a specific team in a specific week\"\"\"\r\n        # Create a query for this specific team, league, season, and week\r\n        query = LeagueQuery(season=season, league=league, team=team, week=week)\r\n        \r\n        # Get the data\r\n        team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(query.to_filter_dict())\r\n        \r\n        if team_data.empty:\r\n            return {\"config\": []}\r\n        \r\n        # Process the data into the expected format for the frontend\r\n        config = []\r\n        \r\n        # Add player details\r\n        for _, row in team_data.iterrows():\r\n            if \"Player\" in team_data.columns and \"Score\" in team_data.columns and \"Opponent\" in team_data.columns:\r\n                player_detail = {\r\n                    \"player\": row.get(\"Player\", \"\"),\r\n                    \"score\": row.get(\"Score\", 0),\r\n                    \"opponent\": row.get(\"Opponent\", \"\")\r\n                }\r\n                config.append(player_detail)\r\n        \r\n        return {\"config\": config}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "class LeagueService:"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 462.5,

            "lineNumber": 463,
            "text": "\r"

            "lineNumber": 464,
            "text": "\r"

          {

            "lineNumber": 470,

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get details for a specific team in a specific week\"\"\"\r\n        # Create a query for this specific team, league, season, and week\r\n        query = LeagueQuery(season=season, league=league, team=team, week=week)\r\n        \r\n        # Get the data\r\n        team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(query.to_filter_dict())\r\n        \r\n        if team_data.empty:\r\n            return {\"config\": []}\r\n        \r\n        # Process the data into the expected format for the frontend\r\n        config = []\r\n        \r\n        # Add player details\r\n        for _, row in team_data.iterrows():\r\n            if \"Player\" in team_data.columns and \"Score\" in team_data.columns and \"Opponent\" in team_data.columns:\r\n                player_detail = {\r\n                    \"player\": row.get(\"Player\", \"\"),\r\n                    \"score\": row.get(\"Score\", 0),\r\n                    \"opponent\": row.get(\"Opponent\", \"\")\r\n                }\r\n                config.append(player_detail)\r\n        \r\n        return {\"config\": config}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {

            "column": 18,

          }

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"Get head-to-head comparison table data for a team vs their opponents in a specific week\"\"\"\r\n        try:\r\n            # Get all matches for the team in this week\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            if team_data.empty:\r\n                return TableData(\r\n                    columns=[],\r\n                    data=[],\r\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\r\n                )\r\n            \r\n            # Get all matches for the opponent teams in this week\r\n            opponent_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            opponent_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_averages_simple(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\r\n            \r\n            ...\n\r\n            \r\n            # Get all teams and weeks\r\n            teams = team_data[Columns.team_name].unique()\r\n            weeks = sorted(team_data[Columns.week].unique())\r\n            \r\n            for team in teams:\r\n                team_week_data = team_data[team_data[Columns.team_name] == team]\r\n                averages = []\r\n                \r\n                for week in weeks:\r\n                    week_data = team_week_data[team_week_data[Columns.week] == week]\r\n                    \r\n                    if not week_data.empty:\r\n                        averages.append(round(float(week_data[Columns.score].mean()), 2))\r\n                    else:\r\n                        averages.append(0)\r\n                \r\n                series_data.add_data(team, averages)\r\n            \r\n            return series_data.to_dict()\r\n            \r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"Error in get_team_averages_simple: {e}\")\r\n            return SeriesData(\r\n                label_x_axis=\"Spieltag\", \r\n                label_y_axis=\"Durchschnitt\", \r\n                name=\"Durchschnitt im Saisonverlauf\", \r\n                query_params={\"season\": season, \"league\": league_name}\r\n            ).to_dict()\r\n\r\n    def get_league_week_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get a simplified league week table with direct data query.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            TableData object with the league standings\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        return self.get_league_table_simple(season=season, league=league, week=week, include_history=False)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_league_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, include_history: bool = False) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get a simplified league table with direct data query.\r\n        Can handle both single week and multiple weeks (history).\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\r\n            include_history: If True, shows all weeks up to the selected week\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            TableData object with the league standings\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        try:\r\n            # If week is not specified, get the latest week\r\n            if week is None:\r\n                week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\r\n            \r\n            # Direct query to get all data for this league and season (both individual and team points)\r\n            # Get individual player data (for scores and averages)\r\n            individual_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=individual_filters)\r\n            \r\n            # Get team bonus data (for team points)\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_bonus_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            # Use individual data for the main league data (scores and averages)\r\n            league_data = individual_data",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_league_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, include_history: bool = False) -\u003e TableData:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get a simplified league table with direct data query.\r\n        Can handle both single week and multiple weeks (history).\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            season: The season identifier\r\n            league: The league name\r\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\r\n            include_history: If True, shows all weeks up to the selected week\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            TableData object with the league standings\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        try:\r\n            # If week is not specified, get the latest week\r\n            if week is None:\r\n                week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\r\n            \r\n            # Direct query to get all data for this league and season (both individual and team points)\r\n            # Get individual player data (for scores and averages)\r\n            individual_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=individual_filters)\r\n            \r\n            # Get team bonus data (for team points)\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_bonus_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            # Use individual data for the main league data (scores and averages)\r\n            league_data = individual_data",

_**Agent**_

Here’s what’s happening and what you should check to fix the missing buttons:

### 1. **Button Population Logic**
- The HTML containers for the buttons (`buttonsSeason`, `buttonsLeague`, `buttonsWeek`, `buttonsTeam`) are present.
- The JS function `initializePage()` is called on `DOMContentLoaded`.
- `initializePage()` fetches available seasons, then calls `loadLeagues()`, which fetches available leagues and (if a league is selected) calls `updateAvailableOptions()` to fetch weeks and teams.

### 2. **Potential Issues**
- **No Season or League Selected:** If neither a season nor a league is selected (i.e., no radio button is checked), `updateAvailableOptions()` will not run, and the week/team buttons will not be populated.
- **No Default Selection:** If the backend returns seasons/leagues but none are marked as checked, the user must manually select one before the dependent buttons appear.
- **URL Parameters:** If the URL does not contain `season` and `league`, no radio button will be checked by default.

### 3. **How to Fix**
- **Auto-select the first available season and league if none are selected.** This ensures the dependent buttons (week, team) are always populated on page load.

---

## **Recommended Fix**

Add logic after populating the season and league buttons to:
- If no season is selected, select the first season and trigger a change event.
- If no league is selected, select the first league and trigger a change event.

This will ensure the week and team buttons are always populated.

---

### **How to Implement**

Add this logic after rendering the season and league buttons in `initializePage`:

```javascript
// After rendering season buttons
const seasonInputs = document.querySelectorAll('input[name="season"]');
if (![...seasonInputs].some(input => input.checked) && seasonInputs.length > 0) {
    seasonInputs[0].checked = true;
    seasonInputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));
}

// After rendering league buttons (inside loadLeagues)
const leagueInputs = document.querySelectorAll('input[name="league"]');
if (![...leagueInputs].some(input => input.checked) && leagueInputs.length > 0) {
    leagueInputs[0].checked = true;
    leagueInputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));
}
```

Would you like me to apply this fix for you?

---

- After rendering the season and league buttons, the code now auto-selects the first available option if none is selected, and triggers a change event.  
- This ensures that the week and team buttons are always populated on page load, even if there are no URL parameters.

_**Agent (2025-07-22 20:47:16Z)**_

You have identified two related but distinct issues:

---

## 1. **When using a direct link with all parameters (season, league, week):**
- All button sections are populated and the correct data is shown.
- **Problem:** Changing the "Season" or "League" button does **not** update the "Team" and "Week" button sections.

### **Why?**
- The event listeners for the "season" and "league" buttons are only triggering content refresh (tables/charts), but **not** re-populating the available weeks and teams.
- The function `updateAvailableOptions()` (which fetches and updates the week and team buttons) is **not called** when you change season or league via the UI.

---

## 2. **When entering the page with no parameters:**
- The "Team" and "Week" buttons **never appear**, even after selecting a season or league.

### **Why?**
- The initial auto-selection logic works, but after the first selection, the event listeners do not call `updateAvailableOptions()` when a new season or league is selected.
- So, the dependent buttons are never populated after the initial load.

---

## **How to Fix Both Issues**

You need to ensure that **whenever the user changes the season or league**, the function `updateAvailableOptions()` is called, so that the week and team buttons are always updated to reflect the new selection.

### **What to do:**
- In your event listener for changes (`document.addEventListener('change', ...)`), add a call to `updateAvailableOptions()` whenever the event target is a "season" or "league" button.

---

### **How to Implement**

Find this section in your JS (in `initializePage`):

```javascript
document.addEventListener('change', event => {
    const name = event.target.name;
    if (name === 'season' || name === 'league' || name === 'week') {
        refreshAllLeagueContent();
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
    if (name === 'team') {
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
    if (name === 'teamView') {
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
});
```

**Change it to:**

```javascript
document.addEventListener('change', event => {
    const name = event.target.name;
    if (name === 'season' || name === 'league') {
        updateAvailableOptions(); // <-- Add this line
        refreshAllLeagueContent();
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
    if (name === 'week') {
        refreshAllLeagueContent();
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
    if (name === 'team') {
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
    if (name === 'teamView') {
        refreshTeamContent();
    }
});
```

---

**This will ensure:**
- Whenever you change the season or league, the available weeks and teams are re-fetched and the buttons are updated.
- The UI will always be consistent, regardless of how you enter the page.

---

Would you like me to apply this fix for you?

---

so about the team colors: i want it to be more sophisticated:
when a new season / league combo is selected, we check the following things:
- remove all team <-> color associations of teams that are not longer there 
- add only for new team the team <-> color association.

lets say we change the season of the same league. many teams will remain there an i want them to keep their color. lets say 2 team have left the league (one level up / down). remove those teams from the team <-> color association. Instead those teams got replaced by two teams new to this league. they need to be assigned colors. by upholding this, one can stick to the teams' colors while browsing thorough the seasons

            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "    console.log('createLineChart invertYAxis argument:', originalInvertYAxis, '-\u003e coerced:', invertYAxis, 'type:', typeof invertYAxis);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "// --- Global chart refresh stub (user should implement) ---\r\nif (!window.refreshAllCharts) {\r\n  window.refreshAllCharts = function() {\r\n    // User: call your chart rendering functions here to update colors\r\n    // Example: updateTableLeagueWeek(); updateTablePosition(data); etc.\r\n    // console.log('refreshAllCharts called');\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Palette Dropdown Handler for Navbar ---\r\ndocument.addEventListener(\"DOMContentLoaded\", function() {\r\n  renderPaletteSwitcher();\r\n  // Palette dropdown in navbar\r\n  document.querySelectorAll('.palette-option').forEach(function(el) {\r\n    el.addEventListener('click', function(e) {\r\n      e.preventDefault();\r\n      const palette = el.getAttribute('data-palette');\r\n      if (palette \u0026\u0026 TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[palette]) {\r\n        currentPaletteName = palette;\r\n        currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[palette];\r\n        // Update navbar text\r\n        const text = el.textContent.trim();\r\n        const paletteText = document.getElementById('currentPaletteText');\r\n        if (paletteText) paletteText.textContent = text;\r\n        if (typeof window.refreshAllCharts === \"function\") window.refreshAllCharts();\r\n      }\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n});\r\n\r\n// --- NOTE: Add \u003cdiv id=\"paletteSwitcher\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e to your HTML where you want the palette switcher to appear. ---\r\n\r\n// --- Update all chart color assignments below ---\r\n\r\n// Example for createLineChart:\r\nfunction createLineChart(data, order, containerId, title, labels, invertYAxis = false, yAxisRange = null) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error('createLineChart: Container not found:', containerId);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Coerce invertYAxis to boolean and log\r\n    const originalInvertYAxis = invertYAxis;\r\n    invertYAxis = (invertYAxis === true || invertYAxis === 'true');\r\n    console.log('createLineChart invertYAxis argument:', originalInvertYAxis, '-\u003e coerced:', invertYAxis, 'type:', typeof invertYAxis);\r\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 101
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 55
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/templates/components/charts.html"
      },
      "score": 0.2910155951976776
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function createScatterChart_vanilla(data, chartId, title, axis_labels) {\r\n    const canvas = document.getElementById(chartId);\r\n    if (!canvas) {\r\n        console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla: Canvas not found:', chartId);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\r\n    // Destroy existing chart if it exists\r\n    if (window[chartId + 'Instance'] instanceof Chart) {\r\n        window[chartId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n    // Collect all points for regression\r\n    const allPoints = Object.values(data).flat();\r\n    const xs = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[0]);\r\n    const ys = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[1]);\r\n    // Calculate regression line\r\n    const n = xs.length;\r\n    const sumX = xs.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r\n    const sumY = ys.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r\n    const sumXY = xs.reduce((sum, x, i) =\u003e sum + x * ys[i], 0);\r\n    const sumXX = xs.reduce((sum, x) =\u003e sum + x * x, 0);\r\n    const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumXX - sumX * sumX);\r\n    const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;\r\n    // Create regression line points\r\n    const minX = Math.min(...xs);\r\n    const maxX = Math.max(...xs);\r\n    const regressionLine = {\r\n        label: 'Regression',\r\n        data: [\r\n            {x: minX, y: slope * minX + intercept},\r\n            {x: maxX, y: slope * maxX + intercept}\r\n        ],\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        borderColor: '#000000',\r\n        borderWidth: 1,\r\n        borderDash: [5, 5],\r\n        pointRadius: 0,\r\n        fill: false\r\n    };\r\n    // Create datasets for each team using palette colors\r\n    const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n    const datasets = teams.map((team, idx) =\u003e ({\r\n        label: team,\r\n        data: data[team].map(point =\u003e ({x: point[0], y: point[1]})),\r\n        borderColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        backgroundColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        pointRadius: 5\r\n    }));\r\n    // Add regression line to datasets\r\n    datasets.push(regressionLine);\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 240,
            "text": "function createScatterChart_vanilla(data, chartId, title, axis_labels) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 241,
            "text": "    const canvas = document.getElementById(chartId);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 242,
            "text": "    if (!canvas) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 243,
            "text": "        console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla: Canvas not found:', chartId);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 244,
            "text": "        return;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 245,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 246,
            "text": "    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 247,
            "text": "    // Destroy existing chart if it exists\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 248,
            "text": "    if (window[chartId + 'Instance'] instanceof Chart) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 249,
            "text": "        window[chartId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 250,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 251,
            "text": "    // Collect all points for regression\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 252,
            "text": "    const allPoints = Object.values(data).flat();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 253,
            "text": "    const xs = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[0]);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 254,
            "text": "    const ys = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[1]);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 255,
            "text": "    // Calculate regression line\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 256,
            "text": "    const n = xs.length;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 257,
            "text": "    const sumX = xs.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 258,
            "text": "    const sumY = ys.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 259,
            "text": "    const sumXY = xs.reduce((sum, x, i) =\u003e sum + x * ys[i], 0);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 260,
            "text": "    const sumXX = xs.reduce((sum, x) =\u003e sum + x * x, 0);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 261,
            "text": "    const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumXX - sumX * sumX);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 262,
            "text": "    const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 263,
            "text": "    // Create regression line points\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 264,
            "text": "    const minX = Math.min(...xs);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 265,
            "text": "    const maxX = Math.max(...xs);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 266,
            "text": "    const regressionLine = {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 267,
            "text": "        label: 'Regression',\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 268,
            "text": "        data: [\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 269,
            "text": "            {x: minX, y: slope * minX + intercept},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 270,
            "text": "            {x: maxX, y: slope * maxX + intercept}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 271,
            "text": "        ],\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 272,
            "text": "        type: 'line',\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 273,
            "text": "        borderColor: '#000000',\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 274,
            "text": "        borderWidth: 1,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 275,
            "text": "        borderDash: [5, 5],\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 276,
            "text": "        pointRadius: 0,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 277,
            "text": "        fill: false\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 278,
            "text": "    };\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 279,
            "text": "    // Create datasets for each team using palette colors\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 280,
            "text": "    const teams = Object.keys(data);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 281,
            "text": "    const datasets = teams.map((team, idx) =\u003e ({\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 282,
            "text": "        label: team,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 283,
            "text": "        data: data[team].map(point =\u003e ({x: point[0], y: point[1]})),\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 284,
            "text": "        borderColor: getTeamColor(team),\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 285,
            "text": "        backgroundColor: getTeamColor(team),\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 286,
            "text": "        pointRadius: 5\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 287,
            "text": "    }));\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 288,
            "text": "    // Add regression line to datasets\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 289,
            "text": "    datasets.push(regressionLine);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 290
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "function createScatterChart_vanilla(data, chartId, title, axis_labels) {\r\n    const canvas = document.getElementById(chartId);\r\n    if (!canvas) {\r\n        console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla: Canvas not found:', chartId);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\r\n    // Destroy existing chart if it exists\r\n    if (window[chartId + 'Instance'] instanceof Chart) {\r\n        window[chartId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n    // Collect all points for regression\r\n    const allPoints = Object.values(data).flat();\r\n    const xs = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[0]);\r\n    const ys = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[1]);\r\n    // Calculate regression line\r\n    const n = xs.length;\r\n    const sumX = xs.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r\n    const sumY = ys.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r\n    const sumXY = xs.reduce((sum, x, i) =\u003e sum + x * ys[i], 0);\r\n    const sumXX = xs.reduce((sum, x) =\u003e sum + x * x, 0);\r\n    const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumXX - sumX * sumX);\r\n    const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;\r\n    // Create regression line points\r\n    const minX = Math.min(...xs);\r\n    const maxX = Math.max(...xs);\r\n    const regressionLine = {\r\n        label: 'Regression',\r\n        data: [\r\n            {x: minX, y: slope * minX + intercept},\r\n            {x: maxX, y: slope * maxX + intercept}\r\n        ],\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        borderColor: '#000000',\r\n        borderWidth: 1,\r\n        borderDash: [5, 5],\r\n        pointRadius: 0,\r\n        fill: false\r\n    };\r\n    // Create datasets for each team using palette colors\r\n    const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n    const datasets = teams.map((team, idx) =\u003e ({\r\n        label: team,\r\n        data: data[team].map(point =\u003e ({x: point[0], y: point[1]})),\r\n        borderColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        backgroundColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        pointRadius: 5\r\n    }));\r\n    // Add regression line to datasets\r\n    datasets.push(regressionLine);\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "        series.push({\r\n            name: team,\r\n            singleAxisIndex: idx,\r\n            coordinateSystem: 'singleAxis',\r\n            type: 'scatter',\r\n            data: seriesData,\r\n            symbolSize: function(dataItem) {\r\n                return Math.max(dataItem[1] / teams.length * 7, 10);\r\n            },\r\n            itemStyle: {\r\n                color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n    const chartOptions = {\r\n        /*title: {\r\n            text: title,\r\n            left: 'center',\r\n            top: 0\r\n        },*/\r\n        tooltip: {\r\n            position: 'top',\r\n            formatter: function(params) {\r\n                return &#96;${teams[params.seriesIndex]}\u003cbr/\u003e\r\n                        ${labels[params.data[0]]}\u003cbr/\u003e\r\n                        Punkte: ${params.data[1]}&#96;;\r\n            }\r\n        },\r\n        singleAxis: singleAxis,\r\n        series: series\r\n    };\r\n    \r\n    chart.setOption(chartOptions, true);  // Added true for clear previous option\r\n    \r\n    // Clean up old resize listener if it exists\r\n    if (container._echartResizeHandler) {\r\n        window.removeEventListener('resize', container._echartResizeHandler);\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Add new resize listener\r\n    const resizeHandler = () =\u003e {\r\n        chart.resize();\r\n    };\r\n    window.addEventListener('resize', resizeHandler);\r\n    container._echartResizeHandler = resizeHandler;\r\n    \r\n    return chart;\r\n}\r\n\r\n\u003c/script\u003e\r\n",

        "originalContents": "        series.push({\r\n            name: team,\r\n            singleAxisIndex: idx,\r\n            coordinateSystem: 'singleAxis',\r\n            type: 'scatter',\r\n            data: seriesData,\r\n            symbolSize: function(dataItem) {\r\n                return Math.max(dataItem[1] / teams.length * 7, 10);\r\n            },\r\n            itemStyle: {\r\n                color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n    const chartOptions = {\r\n        /*title: {\r\n            text: title,\r\n            left: 'center',\r\n            top: 0\r\n        },*/\r\n        tooltip: {\r\n            position: 'top',\r\n            formatter: function(params) {\r\n                return &#96;${teams[params.seriesIndex]}\u003cbr/\u003e\r\n                        ${labels[params.data[0]]}\u003cbr/\u003e\r\n                        Punkte: ${params.data[1]}&#96;;\r\n            }\r\n        },\r\n        singleAxis: singleAxis,\r\n        series: series\r\n    };\r\n    \r\n    chart.setOption(chartOptions, true);  // Added true for clear previous option\r\n    \r\n    // Clean up old resize listener if it exists\r\n    if (container._echartResizeHandler) {\r\n        window.removeEventListener('resize', container._echartResizeHandler);\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Add new resize listener\r\n    const resizeHandler = () =\u003e {\r\n        chart.resize();\r\n    };\r\n    window.addEventListener('resize', resizeHandler);\r\n    container._echartResizeHandler = resizeHandler;\r\n    \r\n    return chart;\r\n}\r\n\r\n\u003c/script\u003e\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r\n    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r\n    if (existingChart) {\r\n        existingChart.dispose();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (order == null) {\r\n        order = Object.keys(data);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Calculate y-axis range if requested\r\n    let yMin, yMax;\r\n    if (yAxisRange === 'auto') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.max(Math.min(...allValues) - 10, 0);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues) + 10;\r\n    } else if (yAxisRange === 'exact') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.min(...allValues);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Create series from the data using the specified order\r\n    const series = order.map(team =\u003e ({\r\n        name: team,\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        data: data[team],\r\n        lineStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team),\r\n            width: 2\r\n        },\r\n        itemStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n        },\r\n        smooth: false\r\n    }));\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "    if (existingChart) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        existingChart.dispose();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "    if (order == null) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "        order = Object.keys(data);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "    // Calculate y-axis range if requested\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "    let yMin, yMax;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "    if (yAxisRange === 'auto') {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "        yMin = Math.max(Math.min(...allValues) - 10, 0);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "        yMax = Math.max(...allValues) + 10;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "    } else if (yAxisRange === 'exact') {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "        yMin = Math.min(...allValues);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "        yMax = Math.max(...allValues);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "    // Create series from the data using the specified order\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "    const series = order.map(team =\u003e ({\r"

            "lineNumber": 127,

            "lineNumber": 138

        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r\n    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r\n    if (existingChart) {\r\n        existingChart.dispose();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (order == null) {\r\n        order = Object.keys(data);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Calculate y-axis range if requested\r\n    let yMin, yMax;\r\n    if (yAxisRange === 'auto') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.max(Math.min(...allValues) - 10, 0);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues) + 10;\r\n    } else if (yAxisRange === 'exact') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.min(...allValues);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Create series from the data using the specified order\r\n    const series = order.map(team =\u003e ({\r\n        name: team,\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        data: data[team],\r\n        lineStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team),\r\n            width: 2\r\n        },\r\n        itemStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n        },\r\n        smooth: false\r\n    }));\r\n",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r\n    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r\n    if (existingChart) {\r\n        existingChart.dispose();\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Initialize new chart instance\r\n    const chart = echarts.init(container, null, {\r\n        devicePixelRatio: window.devicePixelRatio,\r\n        renderer: 'canvas'\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n    const teams = order;\r\n    const singleAxis = [];\r\n    const series = [];\r\n    \r\n    // Configure for each team\r\n    teams.forEach(function (team, idx) {\r\n        const isLastTeam = idx === teams.length - 1;\r\n        \r\n        singleAxis.push({\r\n            left: 40,\r\n            right: 40,\r\n            type: 'category',\r\n            boundaryGap: false,\r\n            data: labels,\r\n            top: (idx * 90) / teams.length + '%',\r\n            height: (90 / teams.length) + '%',\r\n            axisLabel: {\r\n                show: isLastTeam,\r\n                interval: 0\r\n            },\r\n            axisTick: {\r\n                show: false\r\n            },\r\n            axisLine: {\r\n                show: isLastTeam,\r\n                lineStyle: {\r\n                    color: isLastTeam ? '#333' : 'transparent'\r\n                }\r\n            },\r\n            splitLine: {\r\n                show: true,           // Show grid lines\r\n                lineStyle: {\r\n                    type: 'dashed',   // Make them dashed\r\n                    opacity: 0.3,     // Make them semi-transparent\r\n                    color: '#222'     // Light grey color\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        const seriesData = data[team].map((value, index) =\u003e [index, value]);\r\n        \r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r\n    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r\n    if (existingChart) {\r\n        existingChart.dispose();\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Initialize new chart instance\r\n    const chart = echarts.init(container, null, {\r\n        devicePixelRatio: window.devicePixelRatio,\r\n        renderer: 'canvas'\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n    const teams = order;\r\n    const singleAxis = [];\r\n    const series = [];\r\n    \r\n    // Configure for each team\r\n    teams.forEach(function (team, idx) {\r\n        const isLastTeam = idx === teams.length - 1;\r\n        \r\n        singleAxis.push({\r\n            left: 40,\r\n            right: 40,\r\n            type: 'category',\r\n            boundaryGap: false,\r\n            data: labels,\r\n            top: (idx * 90) / teams.length + '%',\r\n            height: (90 / teams.length) + '%',\r\n            axisLabel: {\r\n                show: isLastTeam,\r\n                interval: 0\r\n            },\r\n            axisTick: {\r\n                show: false\r\n            },\r\n            axisLine: {\r\n                show: isLastTeam,\r\n                lineStyle: {\r\n                    color: isLastTeam ? '#333' : 'transparent'\r\n                }\r\n            },\r\n            splitLine: {\r\n                show: true,           // Show grid lines\r\n                lineStyle: {\r\n                    type: 'dashed',   // Make them dashed\r\n                    opacity: 0.3,     // Make them semi-transparent\r\n                    color: '#222'     // Light grey color\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        const seriesData = data[team].map((value, index) =\u003e [index, value]);\r\n        \r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function createLineChart_vanilla(data, chartId, title, labels, reverse_y) {\r\n    const ctx = document.getElementById(chartId).getContext('2d');\r\n    \r\n    // Destroy existing chart if it exists\r\n    if (window[chartId + 'Instance'] instanceof Chart) {\r\n        window[chartId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Create datasets for each team\r\n    const datasets = Object.entries(data).map(([team, values]) =\u003e ({\r\n        label: team,\r\n        data: values,\r\n        borderColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        backgroundColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        tension: 0.1\r\n    }));\r\n    \r\n    window[chartId + 'Instance'] = new Chart(ctx, {\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        data: {\r\n            labels: labels,\r\n            datasets: datasets\r\n        },\r\n        options: {\r\n            responsive: true,\r\n            plugins: {\r\n                title: {\r\n                    display: true,\r\n                    text: title\r\n                },\r\n                legend: {\r\n                    display: false\r\n                }\r\n            },\r\n            scales: {\r\n                y: {\r\n                    reverse: reverse_y,\r\n                    beginAtZero: false,\r\n                    ticks: {\r\n                        stepSize: 1\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "function createLineChart_vanilla(data, chartId, title, labels, reverse_y) {\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "    const ctx = document.getElementById(chartId).getContext('2d');\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "    // Create datasets for each team\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,
            "text": "    const datasets = Object.entries(data).map(([team, values]) =\u003e ({\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "        label: team,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204,
            "text": "        data: values,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205,
            "text": "        borderColor: getTeamColor(team),\r"
          },
